title: phaleria lanceolata
intro: Phaleria lanceolata, also known as the Fraser Fir or Hamanomia, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Hawaii and the Philippines. It is a shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Phaleria lanceolata include its potential to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Phaleria lanceolata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
